name: Our Family's Plum Pudding 1992, II/II
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix all the dry ingredients (plums, sultanas, apples, oranges, almonds, candied peel, baking powder, bread crumbs) in a large bowl.
2. In another bowl, beat eggs and add milk, then gradually mix into the dry mixture until well combined.
3. Add sugar and raisins to form a sticky batter.
4. Place each pudding in its own steaming container on a rack placed over gently boiling water (see note).
5. Cover each kettle with a tight-fitting lid and steam puddings - 7 hours for the larger one filled 2/3 full; 6 hours for the smaller one.
6. Check the water level every hour or so, replenishing with boiling water as needed.
7. When finished steaming, uncover it and let cool.
8. Remove the pudding from the mold if metal, and sprinkle brandy, sherry, rum, or fruit juice over the pudding.
9. Wrap in clean, unbleached muslin, then foil and store in refrigerator to ripen for at least three weeks. Open occasionally and sprinkle more liquor or add it to the pudding cloth.
10. When ready to serve, return to pudding mold and steam again for 1.5 hours.
11. To serve: Place hot pudding on warmed platter. Decorate with holly, candied fruit, or angelica. Warm brandy slightly and pour around the platter. Serve with hard sauce and/or brandy-flavored whipped cream.
Yield: Each pudding serves 10 or more. Makes 5 pounds of pudding or more.
